title: carya × laneyi
intro: Carya × laneyi, also known as the 'Shagbark Hickory' or 'Shellbark', is a deciduous tree that belongs to the walnut family (Juglandaceae). The bark of this tree has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, particularly due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Carya × laneyi include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems. The bark can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
